acceptance_targets: [ch06-craft-001] PASS if the revised identification passage at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:101 states or clearly implies that Gabriel's missing premolar is on the upper left, and no line in the chapter assigns that gap to the right side; FAIL if right-side wording remains or the identifying detail stays internally inconsistent.

[ch06-prose-001] PASS if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:85 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:217 contain no subject-dropped fragments such as "Not talking" and no noun-phrase standalones such as "Silas in shirtsleeves," while still rendering the same burial-memory details in close third; FAIL if the revised text still depends on clipped shorthand rather than complete sentences.

[FBR-001] Pass if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2358-2360 explicitly identifies Gabriel's missing upper left premolar and chapter_06 contains no surviving right-side tooth reference. Fail if the side remains right, is left ambiguous, or no longer matches the exhumation proof in snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4615-4621.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised identification passage at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:101 states or clearly implies that Gabriel's missing premolar is on the upper left, and no line in the chapter assigns that gap to the right side; FAIL if right-side wording remains or the identifying detail stays internally inconsistent.
findings.evidence: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:101, Silas identifies Gabriel by a missing premolar "on the right."
findings.problem: This contradicts the continuity sheet, which establishes Gabriel's canonical dental marker as a missing upper left premolar. Because the tooth gap is one of the chapter's key identification proofs, the side swap is a material continuity break, not a cosmetic wording issue.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the identification beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:101 and, if needed for consistency, the surrounding setup at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:103 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:341 so Gabriel's dental marker matches the canonical upper-left gap and the chapter's later callback still points to the same body.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:85 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:217 contain no subject-dropped fragments such as "Not talking" and no noun-phrase standalones such as "Silas in shirtsleeves," while still rendering the same burial-memory details in close third; FAIL if the revised text still depends on clipped shorthand rather than complete sentences.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:85 breaks into subject-dropped fragments ("Not talking. Not saying...") and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:217 repeats the same clipped-memory shorthand with noun-phrase standalones ("Silas in shirtsleeves... The lamp set on a stone...").
findings.problem: These flashback passages violate the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration. The subjectless fragment chains create stylized shorthand where the chapter otherwise earns its pressure through concrete, grammatical close-third prose, so the memory beats start reading like compressed draft notation rather than finished narrative control.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the clipped flashback runs at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:85 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:217 into grammatically complete sentences that keep Della's sensory pressure on the page. Preserve the bodily facts, but give each beat an explicit subject and verb instead of relying on fragment stacks to manufacture urgency.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2358-2360 explicitly identifies Gabriel's missing upper left premolar and chapter_06 contains no surviving right-side tooth reference. Fail if the side remains right, is left ambiguous, or no longer matches the exhumation proof in snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4615-4621.
findings.problem: Silas identifies Gabriel by a missing premolar "on the right," which contradicts the continuity sheet's canonical marker and the later exhumation sequence, where the missing upper left premolar is treated as decisive proof. Because Gabriel's tooth is one of the book's core forensic identifiers, this wrong-side detail weakens the credibility of every later identification beat.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the identifier exchange at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2358-2362 so Silas names the missing upper left premolar, not the right. Keep the scene's coarse oral texture, but align the bodily marker with the later exhumation language at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4615-4621 so chapter_06 establishes the same forensic fact the book later proves.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into morally compromised caretaking, the bureaucratic brutality of naming, and the physical labor of moving the dead. When an edge case appears, prefer the detail a witness would remember over the phrasing a polite narrator would use. Let tenderness exist inside harsh material without cleaning the harshness off.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If childbirth, exhumation, poverty, sexual history, bodily decay, or violence enters a scene, render it through specific sensation and consequence; summarizing away the hard fact counts as a failed pass, not tasteful restraint.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High overall; contractions are the norm across dialogue, with fuller forms reserved for class performance, legal formality, deliberate emphasis, or a character trying not to lose composure.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate public labor chapters with quieter domestic or inventory chapters, vary scene count and dialogue density, let some chapters turn on information or paperwork rather than confrontation, and ensure no two consecutive chapters share the same dominant mechanism, pace, or emotional temperature.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plainspoken historical Appalachian-Southern English without caricature; concrete, unsentimental, regionally grounded, and alert to how legal language clashes with lived speech.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third centered on Della Bledsoe, intimate enough to track bodily memory and evasive thought while never slipping into other characters' interior monologue as fact
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate muscular work-sentence compression with longer accumulating sentences that carry memory, atmosphere, and moral weight; let cadence tighten in hearings and exhumations, then widen in domestic or reflective passages.
